items.title: Union floats offer to end six-week Boeing Defense strike
items.description: Striking workers at Boeing Defense will vote on Friday on a four-year contract proposed by their union in an effort to end a six-week strike, the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers (IAM) said on Tuesday.  Boeing, however, said the proposal was a waste of time and would prolong the work stoppage in the St. Louis area and nearby Illinois, where the striking workers assemble Boeing's fighter jets, munitions, other military aircraft and wing components for the 777X airliner.  IAM District 837's roughly 3,200 members voted by 57% to reject that earlier offer on September 12.

items.title: $4B Boeing Deal Tangled in Tariffs as Trump Turns Up Heat on India
items.description: Trade talks, war oil, and a blockbuster defense sale collide in New Delhi--can Modi and Trump find common ground?

items.title: Boeing Airplane Orders And Deliveries Take Off Despite Delays
items.description: Boeing stock dips on 777X setbacks, but rising orders and deliveries signal resilience. Learn more about BA stock upside.

items.title: GE Stock Hits Its First All-Time High in Decades. It Took 9,150 Days.
items.description: It took 9,150 days, but  GE  Aerospace stock hit a new all-time high.  GE, which is now GE Aerospace, last hit an intraday high of $289.54 on Aug. 28, 2000, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  Back then, Janet Jackson and N’Sync topped Billboard charts.

items.title: GE Aerospace Enters Into Services Contract With Silk Way West Airlines
items.description: GE signs extended services deal with Silk Way West Airlines, covering GE-powered Boeing freighter fleet.
